subject: A human swimmer underwater being measured against a tall vertical measuring stick / height ruler, with a fish swimming nearby — the visual pun is 'mere man' rendered as a 'mer-man' who is just an ordinary measurable human
description: Underwater scene rendered in cool blues and teals. A bare-chested human swimmer floats vertically mid-frame, body angled head-down toward the right, one arm extended forward. Beside him stands a tall vertical measuring stick or ruler marked with horizontal tick lines, anchored to the seabed. A large fish swims past on the left side of the frame. Bubbles rise through the water column and shafts of light filter down from the surface above. The whole scene is comedic in tone — the gag is that 'Mer Man' is a regular human being measured for height, not a mythological merfolk.
foreground: human swimmer next to a tall measuring stick / ruler
background: underwater murk with a fish, bubbles, and filtered sunlight from above
emotion: playful, absurdist; the swimmer looks calm and posed for measurement
